subject: [PATCH v1 14/17] fuse: use passthrough getattr in setattr suid/sgid handling

reviews.author: Amir Goldstein
reviews.summary:
Reviewer Amir Goldstein expressed concern that the patch is not necessary because the PASSTHROUGH_INO feature would handle suid/sgid kill in a different way, requiring either server-side handling or native handling on passthrough write/trunc ops, and suggested that the explicit non-atomic code in this patch could be avoided.

Reviewer Amir Goldstein expressed concern that the patch may not be necessary if the inode has passthrough getattr enabled, and suggested avoiding this code path altogether.
reviews.raw_body:
I was hoping that this will not be needed because
PASSTHROUGH_INO feature will require HANDLE_KILLPRIV_V2
and then either server handles killpriv or killpriv happens natively
on passthrough write/trunc ops, but never need this explicit non
atomic code to run.

---

Though question - I am hoping that we can avert it by never reaching
this code with PASSTHROUGH_INO enabled.

Thanks,
Amir.

reviews.author: Joanne Koong (author)
reviews.summary: Author agreed to simplify the code by dropping this patch and making handle_killpriv_v2 support explicit, acknowledging that supporting stripping logic on the server side would not be too hard.
reviews.raw_body:
That's a good idea and I think that makes things simplest. It seems
like supporting stripping logic on the server side would not be too
hard. I'll drop this patch and make it explicit that
handle_killpriv_v2 support would need to be enabled to use
PASSTHROUGH_INO.

Thanks,
Joanne

2026-04-22.patch_summary: This patch modifies the behavior of setattr in fuse when handling suid/sgid permissions. When refreshing inode mode, it uses passthrough getattr if the inode has that capability enabled. This change ensures that stale i_mode values are not used during ia_mode calculation.
